title: Project Report: Employee Expense Canvas App — Microsoft Power Platform Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Canvas app for expense submission with SharePoint list backend, approval gallery, and mobile layout.
objectives: Canvas app with form and gallery
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**Employee Expense Canvas App** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Microsoft Power Platform Developer. Duration: 2 weeks. Canvas app for expense submission with SharePoint list backend, approval gallery, and mobile layout. Tools: Power Apps, SharePoint.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Canvas app with form and gallery
Objective: SharePoint list as data source
Objective: Submit and view expenses
Objective: Mobile-optimized layout
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Microsoft Power Platform Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Microsoft Power Platform Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Microsoft Power Platform Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: Published app link; SharePoint list; Mobile screenshot. Skills demonstrated: Canvas apps, SharePoint. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Published app link
Deliverable: SharePoint list
Deliverable: Mobile screenshot
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Published app link and passes the rubric.
